Title: Ablation of glucosinolate accumulation in the oil crop Camelina sativa by targeted mutagenesis of genes encoding the transporters GTR1 and GTR2 and regulators of biosynthesis MYB28 and MYB29

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: CsGTR1-A: LOC104711273; CsGTR1-B: LOC104791006; CsGTR1-C: LOC104780607
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates (GL9, GL10, GL11; total glucosinolates)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antinutritional for human and animal nutrition; also implicated in plant defense against herbivores and pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes glucosinolate accumulation in seeds and roots via transport; loss with CsGTR2 reduces seed glucosinolates by 85–88% and root glucosinolates by ~65–79%
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: all three homeologs expressed much higher in roots than in seeds; CsGTR1-A and CsGTR1-B are predominant in roots (>100-fold higher than seeds)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 targeted mutagenesis of all three homeologs together with CsGTR2; genotyping/sequencing; qPCR; HPLC glucosinolate profiling; embryo/seed coat and silicle wall analyses
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PcUbi (parsley ubiquitin promoter) driving Cas9 in the mutagenesis construct
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth under ambient light; supplemental light in winter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable target for lowering but not fully eliminating glucosinolates in camelina while preserving oil, protein, and fatty acid traits
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no effect on seed oil, protein, or fatty acid composition detected; residual glucosinolates remain, so this strategy alone does not create glucosinolate-free seeds
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: frameshift mutations created likely null alleles lacking transmembrane domains; residual seed glucosinolates suggest de novo embryo biosynthesis and/or alternative transport routes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsGTR1 homeologs are major determinants of glucosinolate transport/accumulation in camelina seeds and roots. 2. Combined mutation with CsGTR2 sharply reduced but did not abolish glucosinolates. 3. Residual glucosinolates in mutant embryos indicate transport-independent contribution to seed accumulation.

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: CsGTR2-A: LOC104762400; CsGTR2-B: LOC104726867; CsGTR2-C: LOC104740346
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates (GL9, GL10, GL11; total glucosinolates)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antinutritional for human and animal nutrition; also implicated in plant defense against herbivores and pathogens
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes glucosinolate accumulation in seeds and roots via transport; loss with CsGTR1 reduces seed glucosinolates by 85–88% and root glucosinolates by ~65–79%
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: all three homeologs expressed about 10-fold higher in roots than in seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 targeted mutagenesis of all three homeologs together with CsGTR1; genotyping/sequencing; qPCR; HPLC glucosinolate profiling; embryo/seed coat and silicle wall analyses
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PcUbi (parsley ubiquitin promoter) driving Cas9 in the mutagenesis construct
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth under ambient light; supplemental light in winter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable target for strong glucosinolate reduction in camelina seed products; useful when partial reduction is sufficient or in combination with biosynthesis regulators
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no effect on seed oil, protein, or fatty acid composition detected; residual glucosinolates remain
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: null mutations produced truncated proteins retaining ETFEK motif but lacking essential transmembrane domains; silicle walls accumulate glucosinolates in mutants, supporting transport role from maternal tissue
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsGTR2 homeologs function with CsGTR1 in glucosinolate transport to seeds and contribute to root glucosinolate accumulation. 2. Combined knockout strongly reduced but did not eliminate glucosinolates. 3. Data support maternal tissue-to-seed transport and indicate additional embryo biosynthesis or alternative transport systems.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates (GL9, GL10, GL11; total glucosinolates)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antinutritional for human and animal nutrition; also implicated in plant defense against herbivores and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CsMAM1 and aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates glucosinolate biosynthetic gene expression; CsMAM1 transcript is ~60-fold lower in csmyb28csmyb29 hextuple mutant embryos than in WT/G3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator; disruption of all three CsMYB28 homeologs together with all three CsMYB29 homeologs abolishes glucosinolate accumulation; CsMYB28 appears predominant especially in roots
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss or reduction of seed and root glucosinolates depending on mutant dosage; together with CsMYB29, complete elimination of glucosinolates in hextuple mutants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: all three homeologs expressed much higher in roots than in green seeds (~10-fold)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 targeted mutagenesis of all homeologs; genotyping/sequencing; qPCR; HPLC glucosinolate profiling in seeds and roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PcUbi (parsley ubiquitin promoter) driving Cas9 in the mutagenesis construct
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth under ambient light; supplemental light in winter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: excellent target to create glucosinolate-free camelina meal and oilseed products; multiplex editing of all homeologs is required in this hexaploid crop
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: seed oil content, seed protein content, and fatty acid composition were not affected in mutants; possible concern about defense reduction discussed but not experimentally shown
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: single/double/triple homeolog combinations show unequal contributions; CsMYB28-A and CsMYB28-B appear particularly relevant for root glucosinolate production
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB28 homeologs are positive regulators of aliphatic glucosinolate biosynthesis in camelina. 2. Combined disruption with CsMYB29 homeologs produced the first glucosinolate-free Brassicaceae crop seeds and roots. 3. CsMYB28 shows stronger contribution than CsMYB29 in some tissues, especially roots.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aliphatic glucosinolates (GL9, GL10, GL11; total glucosinolates)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antinutritional for human and animal nutrition; also implicated in plant defense against herbivores and pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive regulation inferred from loss-of-function phenotypes and orthology; contributes with CsMYB28 to maintain biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supporting positive regulator; all three homeologs must be disrupted together with CsMYB28 homeologs for complete glucosinolate elimination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: contributes to reduction/elimination of glucosinolates when mutated with CsMYB28; alone/homeolog-specific effect appears weaker than CsMYB28 in the tested combinations
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 targeted mutagenesis of all homeologs; genotyping/sequencing; HPLC glucosinolate profiling in seeds and roots
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PcUbi (parsley ubiquitin promoter) driving Cas9 in the mutagenesis construct
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth under ambient light; supplemental light in winter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful multiplex-editing target combined with CsMYB28 to generate glucosinolate-free camelina without affecting oil/protein traits
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: seed oil content, seed protein content, and fatty acid composition were not affected in mutants; possible defense-related concerns discussed but not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: individual homeolog contributions differ; may have tissue-specific roles and possibly stronger roles outside seeds/roots, but direct evidence here is limited
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB29 is a positive regulator contributing to aliphatic glucosinolate accumulation in camelina. 2. Complete elimination of glucosinolates required disruption of all CsMYB29 and CsMYB28 homeologs. 3. Its effect in the tested lines was generally less pronounced than that of CsMYB28 alone.
